zotero.abstractNote: The arrow of time is a fundamental concept in physics, yet its perception by humans remains poorly understood. We investigate whether humans can distinguish between forward and time-reversed autoregressive motion. We find that human observers are surprisingly good at this task, even when the motion is generated by simple linear autoregressive models. We propose a computational model based on the assumption that the human visual system performs causal inference to distinguish between forward and backward motion. Our model accounts for human performance across a range of conditions and suggests that the perception of the arrow of time is linked to the ability to infer the underlying causal structure of dynamic scenes.

extra.Editors: H. Wallach and H. Larochelle and A. Beygelzimer and F. d'Alché-Buc and E. Fox and R. Garnett
